question:
Will the price of Ethereum be above $2,900 on November 21?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: What to Expect From Ethereum Price in November 2025
evidences.content: ## Ethereum Price Setup and Technical Outlook for November On the 2-day chart, ETH shows signs of a hidden bullish divergence — a setup in which the price makes higher lows while the RSI makes lower lows. Between August 21 and October 28, ETH's price formed a higher low, while RSI dropped, indicating that sellers are losing strength. This pattern supports the idea that whale conviction could overpower near-term weakness, validating the broader uptrend. Ethereum is still up over 5% over the past three months — a validation of the said uptrend. Currently, Ethereum trades near $3,860, facing resistance around $4,070 and $4,240. evidences.title: $ETH The ETH/USDT trading pair is one of the most actively - Binance
evidences.content: The ETH/USDT trading pair is one of the most actively traded pairs in the cryptocurrency market. It represents the exchange rate between Ethereum (ETH), the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, and Tether (USDT), a stablecoin pegged to the US dollar. This pair allows traders to buy or sell Ethereum using a stable asset, making it easier to manage risk and navigate market volatility. ETH/USDT is widely available on nearly all major cryptocurrency exchanges and is a common pair for both short-term trading and long-term investment. Because USDT maintains a stable value, it provides a reliable base currency for evaluating the price of ETH. Traders often use this pair for technical analysis, margin trading, and executing arbitrage strategies. evidences.title: Binance API for Historical Crypto Prices | by Zachary Raicik - Medium
evidences.content: In this post, we focus on pulling prices from the `klines` endpoint. The Klines endpoint (/api/v3/klines) is a market data endpoint provided by the Binance API that allows developers to retrieve historical klines/candlestick chart data for a specific symbol (i.e., cryptocurrency) and interval. A `kline` is a representation of a fixed time interval of an asset’s price data, commonly used to represent the opening price, closing price, highest price, and lowest price for a given time period. The Klines endpoint allows developers to retrieve klines data for a variety of time intervals, such as 1 minute, 3 minutes, 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours, 4 hours, 6 hours, 8 hours, 12 hours, and 1 day. ## API Keys [...] logger.error(f"Error making API request: {e}") return response The Binance API returns kline/candlestick data as a list of lists (or an array of arrays). Each inner list represents a single candlestick data point and contains multiple values such as the opening time, opening price, highest price, lowest price, closing price, and so on. However, prefer to have the data stored as a JSON object with more descriptive key-value pairs, you can process the respons...
evidences.query: Binance ETH/USDT 1m candles official API documentation close price kline endpoint

evidences.title: binance_klines: Get kline/candlestick data from Binance in daroczig/binancer: API Client to 'Binance'
evidences.content: # binance\_klines: Get kline/candlestick data from Binance In daroczig/binancer: API Client to 'Binance' View source: R/binance.R | | | --- | | binance\_klines | R Documentation | Get kline/candlestick data from BinanceDescription Get kline/candlestick data from Binance Usage ``` binance_klines( symbol, interval = c("1m", "3m", "5m", "15m", "30m", "1h", "2h", "4h", "6h", "8h", "12h", "1d", "3d", "1w", "1M"), limit, start_time, end_time ) ``` Arguments | | | --- | | `symbol` | string | | `interval` | enum | | `limit` | optional int | | `start_time` | optional POSIX timestamp | | `end_time` | optional POSIX timestamp | Value `data.table` with open-high-low-close values Examples ``` ## Not run: binance_klines('ETHUSDT') binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', limit = 247) [...] binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', limit = 247) binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', start_time = '2018-01-01', end_time = '2018-01-08') ## End(Not run) ``` daroczig/binancer documentation built on March 5, 2024, 4:28 a.m. Related to binance\_klines in daroczig/binancer... daroczig/binancer index README.md R Package Documentation rdrr.io home R language documentation Run R code online Browse...

evidences.content: Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up DiscoverNewsNotificationProfileBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Post Image 2 -----SHARM----- Mar 12, 2025 Follow Report Block User $ETH ETH/USDT refers to the trading pair between Ethereum (ETH) and Tether (USDT) on a cryptocurrency exchange like Binance. ETH (Ethereum): A decentralized platform that runs smart contracts, allowing developers to create and deploy applications (dApps). It's the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, after Bitcoin. USDT (Tether): A stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the US Dollar. It's often used as a stable asset for trading and a safe haven during volatile market conditions. Trading ETH/USDT: [...] Trading ETH/USDT: This pair allows traders to buy or sell Ethereum (ETH) using USDT (Tether). Since USDT is pegged to the US Dollar, it provides a relatively stable trading base compared to more volatile cryptocurrencies. For traders: It’s commonly used for: Speculation: Traders buy or sell ETH based on price movements. Hedging: Converting volatile assets like ETH into USDT to avoid market fluctuations. Liquidity: USDT is widely used for quick entry and exit from positions in Ethereum and other cr...

evidences.title: Ethereum (ETH) Price Prediction 2026 2027 2028 - 2040 - Changelly
evidences.content: Other 2030 projections span a huge range: Cryptopolitan projects a higher range between $14,532 and $18,135. Traders Union offers a more conservative estimate around $5,161–$5,727. Benzinga’s aggregated data spans $1,696 to $6,319. These projections depend heavily on Ethereum’s market share in DeFi, staking growth, and institutional participation. ### Where Will Ethereum Be in 5 Years? Five years from now puts us at roughly 2031. InvestingHaven’s 3-to-5-year model points to a next-cycle upside range of $10,000–$12,000, assuming previous cycle patterns repeat and institutional adoption deepens. [...] 2021: Ethereum’s defining year. NFTs exploded, DeFi TVL surged past $100 billion, and the London hard fork in August introduced EIP-1559, burning a portion of every transaction fee and making ETH deflationary during high activity. ETH hit an all-time high of $4,815 on November 9 and closed the year near $3,680. 2022: A brutal unwinding. Terra/LUNA collapsed in May, triggering contagion that took down Three Arrows Capital and FTX. Despite the market destruction, Ethereum completed its most significant upgrade: The Merge on September 15, transitioning to Proof-of-Stake and cutting ener...

evidences.title: Analyzing Binance ETH-USDT Order Book & Tradebook Data
evidences.content: Total Traded Volume: Sudden spikes in volume often signal increased market participation, potentially driven by liquidations or aggressive buying/selling. Net Trading Pressure: This shows the balance of buy and sell pressure, highlighting which side is dominant during market drops or rallies. Trade Count: The number of trades can reveal shifts in participant behaviour, such as increased retail activity or institutional involvement. Bid-Ask Spread and Volatility: A widening spread indicates reduced liquidity, making trades harder to execute efficiently and increasing slippage. Liquidity-Weighted Price vs. Volume-Weighted Price: Comparing these can expose imbalances in liquidity and trading activity, often leading to higher volatility. [...] 1. Yen Appreciation Begins (July 10th): The first vertical event line corresponds to the start of the Japanese Yen's appreciation on July 10th. Here, we observe a relatively swift 10-15% increase in ETH/USDT, indicating that the market may have been slow to react to the Yen's early rise. We have the API title and description. After that, there's the method and the end point. The total request weight is based on the amount of candles we are asking for. The max allowed number of candles we can import is 1,500. Each interval of candles has [music] its own API weight. Then we have the mandatory and the optional parameters, their type and description. For this case of endpoint, [music] there's a note talking about the start [...] of endpoint, [music] there's a note talking about the start and the end time when they aren't sent. The start and the end time are integer timestamps. Our response will be something like this. evidences.content: def _get_symbols(self) -> List[str]: params = dict() endpoint = "/fapi/v1/exchangeInfo" if self.futures else "/api/v3/exchangeInfo" data = self._make_request(endpoint, params) symbols = [x["symbol"] for x in data["symbols"]] return symbols def get_historical_data(self, symbol: str, interval: Optional[str] = "1m", start_time: Optional[int] = None, end_time: Optional[int] = None, limit: Optional[int] = 1500): params = dict() params["symbol"] = symbol params["interval"] = interval params["limit"] = limit if start_time is not None: params["startTime"] = start_time if end_time is not None: params["endTime"] = end_time endpoint = "/fapi/v1/klines" if self.futures else "/api/v3/klines" raw_candles = self._make_request(endpoint, params) candles = [] if raw_candles is not None:

evidences.title: Secret weapon "Mastering Time Zones" | Trade Oracle X ...
evidences.content: ### 1. Asian Session (UTC 00:00–08:00) 🌸 Who’s Trading? Japan, South Korea, China, and Southeast Asia dominate. Think Binance’s massive APAC user base. What’s Hot? Pairs like BTC/JPY, ETH/USDT, and BNB/USDT see heavy action. Altcoins often pump first here. Why It Matters: Asian markets often set the daily trend. News like China’s regulatory moves or South Korea’s exchange hacks can spark wild volatility. Pro Tip: Follow Binance Square for Asian market sentiment. Use Binance’s K-line charts to spot early breakouts with indicators like RSI or Bollinger Bands. Example: A sudden BTC pump at UTC 03:00 often signals Asian whales accumulating. Jump in early with a limit order on Binance’s spot market. ### 2. European Session (UTC 08:00–16:00) 🏰 [...] ## Why Time Zones Are Your Crypto Superpower 🕒 The crypto market is a global beast, with traders in every corner of the world influencing price action. By syncing your trades with regional market hours, you can: Catch high-volume sessions for tighter spreads and better entries. 📈 Exploit regional news catalysts for explosive moves. 📰 Spot arbitrage opps across Binance’s massive liquidity pools. 💸 Stay ahead of the crowd by trading when oth...

evidences.title: What time does the bitcoin market close? The day begins at - Binance
evidences.content: What are the peak hours for Bitcoin trading? Market Time Conversion Peak trading hours for the market are generally 8am to 4pm local time. These are the trading hours when trading volume in each region is usually the strongest. How much is 1 bitcoin worth? What time of day are cryptocurrency prices lowest? What is the best time to buy Bitcoin? What is the difference between daily and weekly candle? A daily candle is closed and a new candle is opened on the first trade after 00:00:00 UTC, or at 00:00:14 if no trade is completed within 14 seconds. The weekly candle closes when the daily Sunday candle closes. Thank you very much. truly! #BTC☀$BTC See original Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No advice. Binance AI may be used without guarantee.See T&Cs. BTC-0.56% 7 29.3k [...] Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up DiscoverNewsNotificationProfileBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Post Image 2 Zachmoon May 29, 2024 Follow Report Block User What time does the bitcoin market close? evidences.title: Binance Market Data - Amberdata
evidences.content: Tickers: Tickers represent the best bids/asks from an order book. The bid price represents the maximum price that a buyer is willing to pay for an asset. The ask price represents the minimum price that a seller is willing to take for that same asset. ## Binance Historical Trade Data Our Binance trade datasets consist of all tick-by-tick trade data, timestamped, and with the trade direction normalized from the taker side. We have Binance spot data from 2017-08-17 and Binance.US spot data from 2019-09-17. We provide historical trade data via our REST API and real-time trade data via WebSockets for every single asset on Binance. We collect trade data by connecting to Binance’s REST API’s. We poll their market data REST API made publicly available in our API documentation. [...] We offer order book data across all four of our market data sections: spot, swaps, futures and options. In addition to the full order book, we also have snapshots of the order books for the same four sections. These snapshots are 1-minute looks at the order books. If you don’t need the deepest granularity of the full order book, the snapshot endpoints are the next best thing and enable you to see what happen...
